And then lastly, one very important piece of this puzzle is the content exploration experience.
We want to make sure that it's focused on stages so that the person can self-identify and find the most relevant information and guidance for them.

And then beyond that, modern financial ops, and then finally, strategic financial practices.

So when I, when we're building the initial set of.
Topic clusters for Cashflow Desk.
I used the publication context that we created as well as like the main audience personas that we want to target with the website.
And we developed, I mean, we started by analyzing some of the really big players in this space.

So we analyzed close to 4,000 URLs of competitor websites that collectively rank for over 16 million search volume per month in order to identify what are like the top content opportunities for Cashflow Desk that fit in with what we want to do.
And there are different criteria that the keywords that we're targeting, the queries that we're targeting have to meet.
So they have to be low difficulty enough so that a new domain can rank for them.
It should be helpful and actionable to our audience because we're not just talking about basic concepts.
We're targeting people who are dealing with very specific problems they need to answer.
They need answers today.
And we also want to provide them with tools and recommendations for software tools that they can use to make their day-to-day work easier.
So what we did was we filtered out any keywords that have a difficulty score of over 40.
So that left us with still a considerable batch.
And we went ahead and we analyzed these URLs in order to group them into different categories.
You can see here what are the source domains that we used for this analysis.
This is just a start, so we can further enrich this analysis with additional ones.
But we wanted to kind of keep it, we wanted to target like some like behemoths, like QuickBooks that cover many, many different topics.
And they rank for hundreds of thousands of keywords.
And

As well as some smaller players like Airwallex, who target like their content, like they have just like, you know, close to 200 URLs that rank for like lower difficulty keywords, but their content is more actionable in the sense that they are covering, like they cover topics like different tools to support, checkout optimization, how to automate, how to automate finance operations, what kind of software you should use for, to manage chargebacks on credit cards.
So these are all things that our personas are dealing with in our data in their like day-to-day workflows.
So it was important for me to kind of like go to different source domains in order to uncover those topics.
So we ran an analysis of all of these URLs to understand like, what are the top, like what are the topic clusters that they're all targeting collectively, and we can break them down into great.
So, I So, So, I See I
We core tiers.
So the core tiers are core finance operations with like the highest volume.
These tend to be more like higher difficulty, but they are also foundational content that any finance website should be covering in order to build authority for SEO and LLMs.
The second one targets the modern finance stack.
So this type of content would target keywords around accounting software, accounts payable software, software that supports international payments, banking, bank accounts, payment processors.

And the third tier would talk about finance from a more strategic standpoint and talk about different metrics that our personas should be paying attention to, like budgeting, forecasting, but targeting these topics, obviously, from a more like a more

Niche standpoint, so that we're not going after like the huge like volume, super high difficulty keywords, but we're really going after like the more specific use cases related to budgeting, forecasting, managing cash flow, treasury, things like that.
And then we also analyze different industry verticals.
So during the kickoff call, you your conversation with Marcel, he talked about going after different verticals because that allows us to really build authority and for specific industries.
And in the competitor content that we analyze, there are a couple of industries that stand out.
These are construction and trades, SaaS and software companies, e-commerce and retail, restaurants, professional services, healthcare.
And they also talk about target like solo printers and freelancers.
These are like ICPs that we would not.
be targeting with cashflow desks, so we kind of ignored those for the purposes of this strategy.
And we aligned on three verticals that we would like to target as the priority verticals alongside the three core clusters.
And we recommend targeting professional services, e-commerce and retail, and SaaS and software companies.
Those three verticals have enough keyword material for us to work with, and they allow us to go really deep into different use cases, different issues that these industries are dealing with.
And there are also some gaps in these different verticals that we identified in competitor content.
For example, in, you know, the SaaS cluster, we noticed that none of the competitors...
We're talking about how to track MRR or how to bill, like how to support subscription billing.

So there are, you know, things that we could really, we could go super deep while still getting, having higher volume because these industries are obviously quite big and it allows us to kind of hit a good balance of like low difficulty, high volume, high intent keywords.
